title: California Multimedia Evaluation of Gasoline Containing up to 15 percent Ethanol (E15) with Staff Written Summary
content:
On December 11, 2025, the California Air Resources Board (CARB) released the California Multimedia Evaluation of Gasoline Containing up to 15 percent Ethanol (E15), accompanied by a Staff Written Summary. Prepared by an interagency team including CalEPA and the Office of the State Fire Marshal, this extensive evaluation ensures that introducing higher ethanol blends will not adversely affect public health or the environment, specifically analyzing impacts on air, water, and soil.

This publication is a key requirement under Assembly Bill (AB) 30, which immediately authorizes the sale of gasoline blends containing 10.5% to 15% ethanol in California. These sales are permitted until the California Environmental Policy Committee completes its review of the evaluation and CARB either adopts final E15 regulations or determines specific requirements cannot be met. To assist stakeholders, CARB has also posted an FAQ document regarding AB 30 implementation and current reformulated gasoline regulations.
author: Analysts Account
